sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see clearly:
- There are TWO purple squares: one stationary at top [4,30]-[6,32] and one moving at [50,11]-[52,13]
- The moving purple square is now at center (51, 12) - exactly where I clicked!
- The path dots continue: (49,14), (47,16), (45,18), (43,20), (41,22), (39,24), (37,26), (35,28), (33,30), (31,32), (29,34), (27,36), (25,38), (23,40), (21,42), (19,44), (17,46)
- The blue circle is at [11,44]-[19,52]
So the mechanic is: click on the next gray dot along the path to move the purple square there. The goal is to reach the blue circle.
The path pattern: each step goes up 2 rows and right 2 cols.
Current purple: center (51, 12)
Next dots: (49,14), (47,16), (45,18), (43,20), (41,22), (39,24), (37,26), (35,28), (33,30), (31,32), (29,34), (27,36), (25,38), (23,40), (21,42), (19,44), (17,46)
The blue circle starts at row 11-19, col 44-52. The last dot is at (17, 46) which is right at the blue circle's edge.
So I need to click on each remaining dot in sequence. That's 17 more clicks. But the efficiency budget says ~22 target actions total, and I've used 3 already (including the wasted ACTION7). So 19 more actions to reach the goal? That's about right for 17 dots + maybe 1-2 final actions.
Let me batch all the remaining clicks along the path. The centers of the 3x3 purple square need to be clicked. Since the purple square is 3x3, the center is the middle pixel.
Actually wait - I need to click on the NEXT gray dot, not the current purple square position. Let me trace the path:
- Purple is now at (50-52, 11-13), center (51, 12)
- Next dot: (49, 14)
- Then: (47, 16), (45, 18), (43, 20), (41, 22), (39, 24), (37, 26), (35, 28), (33, 30), (31, 32), (29, 34), (27, 36), (25, 38), (23, 40), (21, 42), (19, 44), (17, 46)

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The purple square moved to (50-52, 11-13), center (51, 12).
# I need to click each remaining gray dot along the path to reach the blue circle.
# Path dots (sorted by row, descending = going up-right):
path_dots = [(49,14), (47,16), (45,18), (43,20), (41,22), (39,24), (37,26), (35,28), (33,30), (31,32), (29,34), (27,36), (25,38), (23,40), (21,42), (19,44), (17,46)]

# Build MOUSE actions for each dot
actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in path_dots]

# Execute all at once
result = action(actions)
print(f"Result: {result}")
print(f"Board changed: {result.get('board_changed')}")
print(f"Level completed: {result.get('level_completed')}")
print(f"Done: {result.get('done')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
